channel.title: Epic Fury Live — US-Iran War Tracker
channel.description: Live tracking of the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Real-time updates, verified casualties, evidence, and analysis.

item.title: US sending envoys to Pakistan, raising hopes of talks with Iran’s Araghchi
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi arrives in Islamabad, but Tehran yet to commit to more talks with US delegation.

item.title: Israel continues attacks on Lebanon despite extension of ceasefire
item.description: Israeli forces killed six Hezbollah fighters in an exchange of fire in the area of Bint Jbeil, southern Lebanon.

item.title: Can Europe help the Middle East resolve the war on Iran?
item.description: Al Jazeera’s Rory Challands reports from the EU summit in Cyprus.

item.title: Israeli attacks on Lebanon may violate international law, UN warns
item.description: UN report says Hezbollah rocket attacks on Israel may have breached international humanitarian law.

item.title: Reality check: Israeli ambitions confront US dictates in Iran and Lebanon
item.description: Ceasefires with Iran and Lebanon appear imposed by the US rather than negotiated by Israel, much to latter's unease.

item.title: How Israel targeted and killed Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil
item.description: Amal Khalil was killed while reporting on an Israeli air strike in southern Lebanon during a 10-day ceasefire.

item.title: US has three aircraft carriers in the Middle East for first time since 2003
item.description: As tensions ramp up amid fragile truce, US military says it 'redirected' 34 vessels as part of blockade on Iran's ports.

item.title: PM Sanchez rebuffs US idea of suspending Spain from NATO
item.description: PM Sanchez responded to reports that the US may suspend Madrid from NATO over its opposition to the war on Iran.

item.title: Trump's envoys Witkoff and Kushner to fly to Pakistan for Iran talks
item.description: Iran says its Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is already in Pakistan but that there is no meeting planned between Iran and the US.

item.title: Nato says 'no provision' to expel members after report US could seek to suspend Spain
item.description: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlines options to punish allies over a perceived lack of support for Iran war.
